title.en: How kroeans say "No way, it can't be!" (Expressing disbelief)

description.en: When you hear something that contradicts what you know! Master "에이, 설마요," a phrase that blends denial with a hint of "Maybe not."
description.ko: 내가 알던 사실과 너무 다른 소식을 들었을 때! 부정하고 싶은 마음과 걱정이 섞인 "에이, 설마요" 의 정확한 뉘앙스와 사용법을 배워봅니다.

tags: Situation. Social. News. Denial. Logic. Reaction. Season 2

content.speaker: John
content.text: Mina, I saw a really interesting reaction today. Someone told my friend that our most punctual coworker was skipping work, and he just went, "에이, 설마요."

content.speaker: John
content.text: It didn't sound like a simple 'No' It felt like they were struggling to believe it.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요. "에이 설마요." 는 단순한 부정이 아니에요.
content.translation: Right. "에이, 설마요" is not a simple denial.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 내가 알던 사실과 너무 달라서 믿고 싶지 않을 때 쓰는 말이죠.
content.translation: It's something you say when the news is so different from what you know that you don't want to believe it.

content.speaker: John
content.text: So it's like saying "Maybe not" but with a strong hope that it's false?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 네! "제발 아니었으면 좋겠다." 라는 불안함이 섞인 리액션이에요.
content.translation: Yes! It's a reaction mixed with the anxiety of "I hope that's not the case."

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 오늘은 이 복합적인 감정이 담긴 리액션을 제대로 배워볼게요!
content.translation: Today, we'll properly learn this reaction filled with complex emotions!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 에이~ 설마요. The 에이 part makes it sound like I'm pushing the idea away.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요! "그럴 리가 없다" 라고 손사래를 치는 느낌이에요.
content.translation: Right! It feels like you're waving your hand, saying "There's no way."

insight.title: 에이, 설마요
insight.usage_tip: '에이(Exclamation of denial)' + '설마(Surely not)' + '요(Polite ending)'. It reflects the tension between the news you heard and what you believe to be true.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 에이~ 설마요. 그럴 리가 없어요.
content.translation: Maybe not. That can't be true.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Is there a stronger way to say "That's impossible"?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그럴 땐..., "말도 안 돼요" 라고 해보세요.
content.translation: In that case, try saying "말도 안 돼요".

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 이건 논리적으로 전혀 이해가 안 될 때 쓰는 강력한 리액션이에요.
content.translation: This is a strong reaction used when something makes no sense logically.

insight.title: 말도 안 돼요
insight.usage_tip: '말(Speech)' + '도(Even)' + '안 돼요(Not possible)'. Use this when the situation is completely absurd.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Okay, let's jump in. We are waiting for our friend '수지' who is famous for never being late.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 수지가 아직도 안 오네요. 전화도 안 받아요.
content.translation: John, Suji still isn't here. She's not even answering her phone.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 이상하네요. 수지는 약속을 어긴 적이 한 번도 없잖아요.
content.translation: That's strange. Suji has never broken a promise before.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 혹시 수지가 우리랑 약속한 걸 잊어버린 거 아닐까요?
content.translation: Could it be that Suji forgot her appointment with us?

content.speaker: John
content.text: 에이 설마요! 아까 한 시간 전에도 저랑 확인 문자 주고받았거든요.
content.translation: Maybe not! I even exchanged confirmation texts with her just an hour ago.

insight.title: 에이, 설마요
insight.usage_tip: Use this because you have recent evidence (the text message) that contradicts Mina's guess.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그래요? 그럼 사고라도 난 거 아니에요?
content.translation: Really? Then did she get into an accident? That's unbelievable!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 설마요. 별일 없을 거예요. 조금만 더 기다려 봐요.
content.translation: Surely not. I'm sure it's nothing. Let's wait a little longer.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Now I get it! "에이~ 설마요" is perfect when the news just doesn't fit the person I know.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요. 한국어 리액션은 이렇게 상황과 논리를 모두 담고 있답니다.
content.translation: Right. Korean reactions contain both context and logic like this.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Let's review our "disbelief" reactions one last time.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 에이~ 설마요!
content.translation: Maybe not! / Surely not!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 그럴 리가 없어요!
content.translation: That's impossible!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 말도 안 돼요!
content.translation: That's nonsense!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 진짜 아니겠죠?
content.translation: It's not true, right?

content.speaker: John
content.text: 아닐 거예요!
content.translation: It won't be true!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 여러분도 오늘 믿기 힘든 소식을 들었을 때, "에이, 설마요" 로 여러분의 마음을 표현해 보세요.
content.translation: Everyone, when you hear unbelievable news today, express your heart with "에이, 설마요."

content.speaker: John
content.text: That's all for today. See you in the next episode.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 다음에 또 만나요. 안녕~!
content.translation: See you again next time. Bye~!

vocabulary.example: 설마 저 사람이 범인은 아니겠지? (Surely that person isn't the criminal, right?)
갑자기 눈이 내리다니 말도 안 돼요. (It's unbelievable that it's suddenly snowing.)
성실한 존이 지각을 할 리가 없어요. (There is no way that diligent John would be late.)
그는 사실이 아니라고 강하게 부정했어요. (He strongly denied that it was not true.)
소식이 없어서 마음이 정말 불안해요. (I'm really anxious because there's no news.)
제 생각이 맞다는 확실한 확신이 있어요. (I have a clear conviction that my thinking is right.)
